Question: Given an array arr of lowercase strings, determine if the strings can be chained together to form a circle.

A string X can be chained together with another string Y if the last character of X is the same as the first character of Y. If every string of the array can be chained with exactly two strings of the array(one with the first character and the second with the last character of the string), it will form a circle.
For example, for the array arr[] = {"for", "geek", "rig", "kaf"} the answer will be Yes as the given strings can be chained as "for", "rig", "geek" and "kaf"
Explaination: These strings can't form a circle because no string has 'd'at the starting index.
Explaination: These strings can form a circle of strings.
1. Create a directed graph where each vertex represents a character (first and last characters of the strings). [1 mark]
2. For each string in the array, add a directed edge from the first character to the last character of the string. [1 mark]
3. Check if every vertex in the graph has an in-degree strictly greater than its out-degree (necessary condition for Eulerian circuit). [1 mark]
4. Verify that all non-zero degree vertices form a single strongly connected component (using DFS or similar algorithm). [1 mark]
1. Count the frequency of each character appearing at the beginning and at the end of all strings. [1 mark]
2. For each unique character, check if its frequency at the beginning of strings equals its frequency at the end of strings. [1 mark]
3. Ensure that the graph formed by the strings is a bipartite graph (can be checked by performing BFS coloring). [1 mark]
4. Return true if both conditions are met, otherwise return false. [1 mark]
1. Create an adjacency list where each node represents a string, and edges represent the possibility of chaining two strings. [1 mark]
2. For each string, identify all other strings that can be chained after it (first char of current = first char of next). [1 mark]
3. Check if each string can be chained with exactly two other strings (one before and one after). [1 mark]
4. Verify that these chains form a linear path without any cycles (by traversing the adjacency list). [1 mark]
